behavior_summary: Provides gameplay handlers for a table object. The primary 'U8P_WTABLEEW::look' handler makes the object produce a short description string ('table ') to the viewer and prepares the object for examination by setting item info and process-exclude state.
Includes a hit handler (gotHit) that, under a specific condition on its second argument (inferred sentinel), changes the object's visual frame to 1 to reflect a reaction to being hit.
All external interactions are local to the item: emitting text via Item::bark and changing appearance via Item::setFrame. No global game flags are modified by these handlers in this excerpt.
notable_control_flow: U8P_WTABLEEW::look: set item info/process-exclude → invoke Item::bark(this, "table ") → free temporary string → push/handle retval/implies → suspend briefly (typical U8P_WTABLEEW::look handler lifecycle).

U8P_WTABLEEW::gotHit: set item info/process-exclude → examine second parameter; only when it matches the inferred sentinel condition does it call Item::setFrame(this, 1); otherwise returns without changing frame.
open_questions: Exact meaning and numeric value of the 'second parameter' to gotHit that triggers the frame change (disassembly implies a sentinel like -1/0xFFFF but this is inferred).
Whether U8P_WTABLEEW::look receives an explicit viewer/process id (pid) as a parameter or uses an implicit 'this' PID for Item::bark in this engine variant (inferred but not explicit in the excerpt).
Precise semantics and id of the 'set info' / 'process-exclude' operation applied at the start of handlers (what info key is set and how exclusion influences later processing).
Why the U8P_WTABLEEW::look handler performs the implies/pop sequence before suspend (engine control detail—likely standard event return behavior but implementation specifics are unclear).

main_function.name: U8P_WTABLEEW::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the object's examination by a player/actor: prepare object info/exclusion state and emit the short description string 'table ' via Item::bark, then complete the handler lifecycle (return value handling and suspend).
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_WTABLEEW::look) — triggered when a player/actor looks at or examines the table object (area/interaction event).

function_map.name: U8P_WTABLEEW::look
function_map.role: Primary 'U8P_WTABLEEW::look' event handler for the table object; prepares state and emits description text.

function_map.name: U8P_WTABLEEW::gotHit
function_map.role: Hit event handler: conditionally changes the object's visual frame to indicate being struck (guarded by a specific second-parameter check).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to present a short text message or 'bark' from the item (used for object descriptions and short messages).

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to change an item's animation/visual frame (used by gotHit to set frame 1).

external_calls.purpose: Emit the table's short description string 'table ' to the viewer/process (UI feedback for examine/U8P_WTABLEEW::look).
Change the table object's visual/animation frame to 1 when the hit handler's second-parameter sentinel condition is met (visual reaction to hit).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides gameplay handlers for a table object. The primary 'U8P_WTABLEEW::look' handler makes the object produce a short description string ('table ') to the viewer and prepares the object for examination by setting item info and process-exclude state.
